title: State Police Sgt. Scott Quigley indicted in deadly Woburn crash
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: State Police Sgt. Scott Quigley indicted for motor vehicle homicide in a 2023 Woburn crash that killed Angelo Schettino.
Quigley allegedly drove drunk, causing his cruiser to cross traffic and hit a van carrying Schettino back to his group home.
Suffolk County DA's Office handling the case due to Quigley's ties to Middlesex; arraignment in Middlesex Superior Court pending.

title: Framingham’s iconic ‘castle’ hotel has entered a new era. Take a look inside.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The former Sheraton Framingham Hotel has become The Framingham Hotel Collection, a tri-brand Marriott Bonvoy property.
The 371-room campus includes Renaissance Framingham Hotel, Fairfield Inn & Suites, and TownPlace Suites with modern amenities.
Guests can choose from three dining options, including Malone’s Irish Pub & Social and the New England-inspired Provisions Hearth & Kitchen.

title: With travel concerns rising, are you rethinking flights?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Concerns rise over TSA staffing at Logan Airport due to Department of Homeland Security shutdown.
Union leaders warn TSA staffing issues could cause delays as summer travel season approaches.
Deadly crash at LaGuardia Airport raises safety concerns, linking staffing and technology failures.

title: This Boston hotel is giving music lovers a reason to travel
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Omni Boston Hotel at Seaport offers an All Access Pass package, including sold-out concert tickets.
New biometric system at Logan Airport allows TSA PreCheck flyers to verify identity with facial recognition.
Coastal Maine Botanical Garden in Boothbay named among best botanical gardens in the U.S. by USA Today.

title: Tom Brady reveals that he has ‘explored a lot of different things’ about making an NFL comeback
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tom Brady explored a potential NFL return, but NFL rules prevent active players from holding team ownership.
Brady, 48, is a minority owner of the Raiders and would need to divest to become an active player.
Despite exploring comeback options, Brady remains happily retired, focusing on ownership and broadcasting ventures.

title: Police release bodycam footage of Plymouth butcher knife standoff
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Plymouth police released footage of a standoff involving a man holding his estranged wife at knifepoint.
The incident occurred on Tuesday night when the suspect broke into a home on Clark Road and held a woman hostage.
After 45 minutes of negotiation, the suspect surrendered and faces over a dozen criminal charges.

title: MLB debuts automated strike challenge system for 2026 season. Here’s what you need to know.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MLB introduces the Automated Ball-Strike Challenge System for the 2026 season, allowing real-time challenges of umpire calls.
Players on each team have two challenges per game, retaining them if successful; additional challenges available in extra innings.
The system is available in all standard MLB stadiums but not for special events like the Mexico City Series and Field of Dreams game.

title: Kelsey Fitzsimmons acquitted of assault charge
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons was acquitted of assault with a dangerous weapon after a bench trial in North Andover.
Judge Jeffrey Karp ruled that prosecutors did not prove beyond a reasonable doubt Fitzsimmons pointed a gun at Officer Patrick Noonan.
The case involved an incident where Fitzsimmons allegedly tried to commit suicide after being served with a restraining order.

title: OKC’s Shai Gilgeous-Alexander impressed by ‘playoff feel’ in loss to Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics defeated the Thunder, advancing to second place in the Eastern Conference, half a game ahead of the Knicks.
Shai Gilgeous-Alexander praised the 'playoff feel' at TD Garden, emphasizing the Celtics' strong culture and competitive edge.
The regular-season series between Celtics and Thunder ended 1-1, hinting at a potential NBA Finals rematch.

title: Trooper arrested in Boston for OUI was allegedly ‘slumped’ at the wheel with High Noon in cupholder
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Massachusetts State Police Trooper Donovan Preston was arrested for OUI and open container after being found slumped in a car.
Preston's vehicle was stopped on Herald Street in Boston with a running engine and an open High Noon in the cupholder.
Preston pleaded not guilty and was released on personal recognizance; he faces internal discipline and is due in court May 13.
